paper_title: Optimization of non-thermal plasma process to remove methyl blue towards application in wastewater treatment

entities.description: The main field of research focusing on treating industrial sewage.
The research problem of eliminating methyl blue dye from aqueous solutions.
Chemical treatment procedures to remove organic compounds through oxidation.
A type of non-thermal plasma used for the degradation of organic pollutants.
Response surface methodology and central composite design used for experimental design and optimization.
Mathematical model used to fit experimental data and predict responses.
Equipment used to analyze the concentration of methyl blue at 663 nm.
The set of numerical results obtained from the plasma treatment experiments.
The efficiency of methyl blue removal at optimal conditions.
The final remaining concentration of dye after treatment.
The optimized power setting for the plasma process.
The optimized rate of liquid flow through the system.
The optimized rate of air supply for ozone generation.
The optimized duration of plasma treatment.
Statistical measure of how close the data are to the fitted regression line.
